metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A semi-aquatic river beastfolk city where Riverfolk Lockwheel Palace of Turning Waters, Lock House Beacon, Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Dunmere Locks should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Dunmere Locks organizes power through Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel, public response through Lock House Beacon, civic memory through Riverfolk Lockwheel Palace of Turning Waters, and daily life through S1 Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, S2 Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Medical / Healing Civic Hall, S5 Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Teak, Red Brick, Limestone Blocks, Silk, Arcane Glow, Riverside, Floodplain, Canal Basin, Beast Bonding, Ancestor Guidance, Tide Sorcery, and exact anchors including Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel, Lock House Beacon, Riverfolk Lockwheel Palace of Turning Waters, Nursery Basin, Grain Hall, Healing Hall, Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Canal Reflections Monument, Waterwheel Court Nook, Granary / Food Market Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A semi-aquatic river beastfolk civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Riverfolk Lockwheel Palace of Turning Waters, Lock House Beacon, Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel, and section anchors such as Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, Civic Services Hall, Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall.

canonLocks: Dunmere Locks uses city code DLS.
The Wonder is Riverfolk Lockwheel Palace of Turning Waters.
The Beacon is Lock House Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.

The source-backed section spine is: S1 Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, S2 Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Medical / Healing Civic Hall, S5 Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Dunmere Locks as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Dunmere Locks's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

skyline: Riverfolk Lockwheel Palace of Turning Waters

colorLanguage: local textile, stone, metal, vegetation, or water colors from the image package

civilization.people: The population should read as semi-aquatic river beastfolk and city-specific: citizens occupy streets, shops, public thresholds, and landmark approaches as people who understand the local civic order.

households tied to local district obligations
civilization.governance: Government is centered on Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel. Its authority should be visible through courts, records, public access routes, civic lamps, guards or attendants, and the way citizens respond to D13.
civilization.religion: Religious or ritual life should only be emphasized where source anchors support it, especially names such as none explicitly dominant in the current anchors.

streets.appearance: Streets should show Dunmere Locks's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, S2 Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Medical / Healing Civic Hall, S5 Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall. Keep roads, plazas, stairs, thresholds, lamps, signs-without-readable-text, props, and civilians aligned to the source-backed materials and landmarks.
streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.
sounds: landmark-specific sounds from water, bells, animals, machines, or crowds when source-backed
smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

common: street services tied to section anchors
exports: specialized tools or ritual goods as supported by anchors
imports: dyes, oils, spices, timber, stone, metal, glass, or cloth as needed by the architecture

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
